purpose: Define how downstream CadStruct-MoE scene-graph integration should compare final symbol policies without changing the default runtime policy.

oracle_contract_export.role: Maps expected_json.symbol_candidates into ExpertPrediction(family=symbol, expert=symbol_fixture) and fuses a scene graph.

real_expert_runner.role: Builds RoutedCandidate rows from expected_json.symbol_candidates, runs SymbolFixtureExpert, and writes normalized ExpertPrediction JSONL.

fusion_v2.role: Fuses real upstream expert outputs and evaluates node/edge F1; candidate generation is internal to the script today.

integration_contract.input_schema: symbol page predictions JSONL with row_id and predicted_symbols[{bbox,label,label_id,score,tile_id,...}]
integration_contract.adapter_target: RoutedCandidate/ExpertPrediction compatible symbol_candidates for CadStruct-MoE fusion
integration_contract.default_behavior: Use v28 unless --symbol-policy or config explicitly selects p070, p076, or p078.
integration_contract.matching_note: Current MoE smoke rows do not carry the detector row_id; adapter must use a stable row_id manifest or image-path mapping before an end-to-end run is valid.

smoke_eval_plan: Create a small manifest that maps MoE smoke records to detector row_id/image_path.
Convert v28 and p076 page predictions into symbol_candidates or ExpertPrediction rows with identical non-symbol inputs.
Run fusion on both variants and compare scene-graph node F1, symbol-family node F1, invalid graph rate, and warning deltas.
Keep v28 default unless downstream P076 improves symbol-family F1 without materially increasing invalid graphs or warnings.

promotion_gate.reopen_symbol_work_only_if: Downstream deltas show symbol quality is still the bottleneck after p076/p078 comparison.
